DISCOVERY 610, PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES -- MARY E. CLAUGUS APPEARING FOR PETITIONERS. AFTER HEARING TENATATIVE RULING ADOPTED AS AMENDED: MITON IS GRANTED, TO THE EXTENT THAT THE REQUESTS ASK FOR DOCUMENTS FROM 1982 TO THE PRESENT. AFTER REVIEWING THE PETITION BY THE TRUSTEES FOR AN ORDER DETERMINING CONSTRUCTION AND THE SECOND AMENDED AND ANNOTATED OBJECTIONS TO ACCOUNT AND THE PETITION BY RESPONDENT, THE COURT FINDS THAT THE DOCUMENTS ARE RELEVANT TO THE SUBJECT MATTER OF THIS ACTION AND REASONABLY CALCULATED TO LEAD TO THE DISCOVERY OF ADMISSIBLE EVIDENCE. THE REMAINING OBJECTIONS ARE OVERRULED. IF ANY DOCUMENTS ARE WITHHELD PURSUANT TO A CLAIM OF PRIVILEGE, THE TRUSTEES AND HELLER BENEFICIARIES SHALL PREPARE A PRIVILEGE LOG.
